feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Readiness checks for BW/4 HANA upgrades
entry.content:
Hello PERSON_NAME,
 
We are upgrading our system on BW/4 PERSON_NAME DIGITS to BW/4 PERSON_NAME DIGITS.
Could you please confirm if readiness check is supported/required for BW/4 PERSON_NAME upgrades.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Readiness checks for BW/4 HANA upgrades
entry.content:
SAP Readiness Check for SAP BW/4HANA supports all releases that are suitable for conversion to SAP BW/4HANA:

  • Shell conversion for SAP BW 7.0 or higher on Any-DB
  • Remote conversion for SAP BW 7.30 or higher on Any-DB
  • In-Place conversion for SAP BW 7.50 or higher on SAP HANA

Thus SAP Readiness Check is not supported BW/4 HANA 2021 to BW/4 HANA 2023. Only support ERP upgrade to BW/4HAN and SAP BW/4HANA upgrade to S/4HANA cloud.

entry.title: VIM: single document type for multiple channels
entry.content:
Dear gurus: 

We would like to know if it is possible to assign a single VIM document type for multiple channels. 

The reason: we have 2 different sources for the generation of DPs (invoices) but we would like to avoid having duplicated document types. Not sure if it is feasible to include some logic to differentiate the mappings for each channel and manage it with a single document type.

Could anybody help us with this topic, please?

Thanks in advance.

Best regards,

J

entry.title: want to sync Users and groups from ADFS to customer IAS
entry.content:
Hi, Expert!

One of my customers is building custom application on the BTP environment, and they are going to setup SSO Architecture as well.  

Now, they are using ADFS as SSO solution,  they want to sync Users and groups from ADFS to customer IAS.  

 

I need to clear Customer inquiries for IPS functionality.

But I’m not sure that IPS is supported ADFS to synchronize users and groups from ADFS to customer IAS.

There are many articles for Azure AD, But nothing for ADFS.

For example, I found your blog Identity Provisioning: How to Get Users Based on G... - SAP Community, But it explains only for Azure AD.

So, I’m wondering if IPS is supported ADFS as well.  If yes, could you please some URL about it?

 

Thanks ! Best regards,

Jihoon jihoon.kim01@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Creating api to send mail
entry.content: Hi, what did you tried, where do you have difficulty with this?

entry.title: Re: Error executing action: Could not fetch client creden...

Soumya

entry.title: How to do Lease Accounting in Cloud 2402
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are trying to do the lease accounting configuration in cloud 2402 version, But we are unable to find any proper setup for it. Can anyone help us with any documentation or SAP links. 

FIN Asset Accounting SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management 

Thanks,

Harshali Morekar

entry.title: Re: no exchange rate type defined for country/region kr
entry.content: issue fixed, and user need to 'You must use tcode OY01 to maintain the fields 'Country currency' and 'Exchange rate type'.', close this question.

entry.title: Specifiy staging method after PMR creation
entry.content:
Hello,

in a PMR we have a component that can either be picked from a silo or from a container in a pallet storage. 

When the production order is created, it is not yet known whether the component should be removed from a silo or a container, but only when it is released.

We would like to use different staging methods for both cases:

1) Silo: Not relevant for Staging

2) Container: Cross Order Staging 

How can we achieve this? Do we always need to create/change the entry in /SCWM/PSASTAGE before production order release, depending on whether the stock should be picked from the silo or container?

Or is it also possible to create the component item in the PMR initially without staging method (and goods movement bin) and to specify the staging information later? 

Thank you.

Best regards,

Julian

entry.title: SAP DMC - BOM Formula - Use two formulas in the same BOM
entry.content:
Dear SAP DMC community,

I have encountered an issue while working with formulas in SAP DMC and would appreciate your guidance. I have created two formulas to calculate the quantity for two components in the BOM. My intention is to apply one formula to each component, but I'm facing an issue. When I choose the formula for one component, it seems to be applied to other components as well.

Specifically, my questions are:

  1. Is it possible to assign different formulas to different components within the same BOM?
  2. Alternatively, is there a way to choose the formula that I want to apply to each component during consumption?

Here's an example scenario:

BOM "X"

  • Component A => Formula A
  • Component B => Formula B
  • Component C => No formula assigned
  • Component D => No formula assigned

Formula A involves quantity calculation with a link to component C, while Formula B involves quantity calculation with a link to component D.

I would appreciate any insights or recommendations on how to address this issue and ensure that each component in the BOM can have its own unique formula.

It's an integration between SAP S/4 HANA On premise 2023 --> SAP DMC

Thank you for your assistance.

Best Regard,

Ayoub

entry.title: Managing Security Roles across S/4HANA Public Cloud and SuccessFactors
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Public Cloud, our organisation have configured Finance and Purchasing whilst the HCM and Payroll functionalities are supported in SuccessFactors EC and ECP. We would like to make use of the Positions in HCM-OM to maintain financial delegations (authority) to drive the workflows in HCM, Finance (e.g. Journal posting, payment proposal, etc.) and Procurement (e.g. PO approval). Traditionally, in non-cloud S/4HANA solution implementation, we build security roles (from TCodes => Single Role => Composite Roles => Enterprise Business Roles such as Financial Accountant or a Purchasing Officer just to quote a couple of examples that would drive the workflows in Purchasing or Finance. In SuccessFactors, we understand the access is controlled by Role-Based Permission (RBP) which provides access for example to a Manager to approve a leave request. With security roles spread across both S/4HANA for Finance & Purchasing modules while HCM & Payroll access is maintained via SuccessFactors RBP, can anyone please explain or share your experience how are security roles / access being managed across both S/4HANA Public Cloud and SuccessFactors EC. 

As we are new to S/4HANA Public Cloud, can we use SAP GRC (Governance, Risk & Compliance) to manage and synchronise those security roles across both S/4HANA and SuccessFactors? If not what would / should be the solution be in place of GRC? Can someone confirm if DRC (Document & Reporting Compliance) the new GRC on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? 

We would appreciate if you could provide insights on how security access / roles are managed across both S/4HANA and SuccessFactors.

Many thanks.

entry.title: Meta chain loading failed suddenly, Reported error "Error message from the source system".
entry.content:
Hi, 

Meta chain failed with the error massage and when I repeat second time it run successfully. 
Find the error snaps and suggest to resolve.

Both highlighted were failed.

 

 

 

Regards//
Sitanshu

entry.title: Re: Using PAL Procedures embeded in SQL Script (anonymous...
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

Here is an example of anonymous block running APL forecast without Declare.

DO BEGIN     
 header   = select * from #HEADER_APL;       
 config   = select * from #CONFIG_APL;    
 var_desc = select * from #DESCCRIPTION_APL;              
 var_role = select * from #ROLES_APL;  
      
 "SAP_PA_APL"."sap.pa.apl.base::FORECAST" ( 
 :header, :config, :var_desc, :var_role,
 'USER_APL','SERIES_IN', 'USER_APL','SERIES_OUT', 
 out_log, out_summary, out_indicators );
    
 select * from "USER_APL"."SERIES_OUT" order by 1;
END;


Cheers,

Marc

entry.title: Re: [BUG] blog shows up under untagged tag
entry.content: Hi @Johan_H - we are looking into it.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector with terraform
entry.content: thanks for the answer will be looking in to it 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAC message UQM_OPTIMIZED_USER_FRIENDLY_PERFORMANCE
entry.content: I get the same. It seems to be after trying to set the height or width to 'auto' on a chart?

entry.title: Unexpected response using the API Gateway ExportPDFData
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are in the process of developing a client portal that leverages the Service Layer to fetch invoices, incoming payments, and other customer-related information. Additionally, we aim to automate the process of exporting this data as invoices. However, we've encountered an issue.

When attempting to use the ExportPDFData endpoint to retrieve invoices and incoming payments, we receive unexpected responses, as demonstrated in the attached images. In contrast, when applying ExportPDFData to Crystal Report Document Types, everything functions as expected, as shown in attached images.

Could someone explain the difference between the various Document Types regarding Layout and Sequence? Specifically, why do Crystal Report document types work as expected, while others do not?

Thank you for your time.

@ANKIT_CHAUHAN

entry.title: how to removes duplicates entries in query generator in sap b1
entry.content:
SELECT distinct T0."ItemCode" AS "Itemcode",T1."U_UTL_BTNO",T0."DocDate",
T0."Dscription" AS "Item",T3."InvntryUom" as "UoM", T4."ItmsGrpNam",

/*select d."ItmsGrpNam" from oitb d where d."ItmsGrpNam"=[%3]*/

T2."PlannedQty" AS "Std. Qty",

T0."Quantity" AS "Issued Qty",

(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ) AS "Returned Qty",

(T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ) ) AS "Consumption",

(T2."PlannedQty"-(T0."Quantity"- (T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ) )) AS "Excess/Shortage",T5."Price",

((T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ))*T0."Price") AS "Item rate",

(T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"-T2."IssuedQty" )) * ((T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty"))*T0."Price") AS "Std Value (FIFO)",

 

(CASE WHEN ((T2."PlannedQty"-(T0."Quantity"- (T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ))) * ((T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty"))*T0."Price")) = 0
THEN (T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"-T2."IssuedQty" )) * ((T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty"))*T0."Price")
ELSE ((T2."PlannedQty"-(T0."Quantity"- (T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty" ))) * ((T0."Quantity"-(T0."Quantity"- T2."IssuedQty"))*T0."Price"))
END) AS "Actual Value (FIFO)"


FROM IGE1 T0 INNER JOIN OWOR T1 ON T0."BaseRef"= T1."DocNum" and T0."BaseType"=T1."ObjType"

INNER JOIN WOR1 T2 ON T1."DocEntry" = T2."DocEntry" AND T2."ItemCode" = T0."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN OITM T3 ON T0."ItemCode" = T3."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN OITB T4 ON T4."ItmsGrpCod" = T3."ItmsGrpCod"
INNER JOIN PDN1 T5 ON T5."ItemCode"= T2."ItemCode"
INNER JOIN OPDN T6 ON T6."DocEntry" = T5."DocEntry"

WHERE T4."ItmsGrpCod" IN ('100','101')

AND T0."DocDate" >= [%0] AND T0."DocDate" <= [%1] AND T1."U_UTL_BTNO" =[%2] AND T4."ItmsGrpNam"=[%3]

entry.title: APC (TCP/Websockets) connection for SAP BTP ABAP environment
entry.content:
Dear SAP & community!

I found no documentation about ABAP Push Channels on the BTP ABAP environment. Are there any plans to include this feature in the near future?

We, as an implementation partner, have to connect to 3rd party systems which offer only TCP/IP connections very often.

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: SAP SIGNAVIO: Process Documentation Templates in Exce...
entry.content:
Hello Tiago,

There is no option to create Process documentation on an excel. Once you've completed your work within Signavio, you can export multiple reports in Excel format. 

Please let me know if this addresses your question or if you need further assistance on your specific case.

Best regards,
